purpose: Hardware and schematic witness map for the first AS physical-evidence path. This map identifies the PRC anchors that a later schematic or simulation artifact must preserve before AS claims it honors PRC's hardware commitments.

witnesses.summary: Names the Universal Cell as the homogeneous primitive for PRC and requires reversible logic, asynchronous operation, explicit signal and power routing, and organic component-wise reconfigurability.
witnesses.simulation_constraint: Any first schematic or simulation must preserve the UC as the least semantic element carrying all four PRC hardware requirements.
witnesses.next_as_action: Use these four requirements as acceptance criteria for the first hardware/schematic artifact.

witnesses.locus: Geometrically explicit circuit definition and triangular RLEM lattice construction
witnesses.summary: Defines GELCs as circuits where wire geometry is part of the formal circuit definition and describes triangular RLEM nodes whose labels and memory states define the circuit.
witnesses.simulation_constraint: The first schematic should make wire geometry, node labels, ports, and memory orientation explicit rather than treating routing as an implicit drawing convention.
witnesses.next_as_action: Derive a single triangular RLEM node diagram and port orientation vocabulary from this source before drawing larger circuits.

witnesses.summary: Local witness for the RLEM universality foundation that PRC translates into geometrically explicit Universal Cell/GELC form.
witnesses.simulation_constraint: Do not treat the AS schematic as a new primitive logic family; its logic direction must remain tied to RLEM-style reversible memory elements.
witnesses.next_as_action: Use RLEM memory and port direction as the logical behavior layer beneath the AS schematic vocabulary.

witnesses.summary: Connects RLEM/GELC nodes to switchable latching circulators as a possible hybrid active-electric/passive-EM physical implementation.
witnesses.simulation_constraint: The first hardware note must mark circulator physics as candidate physical evidence, not as already verified PRC hardware.
witnesses.next_as_action: Separate symbolic RLEM behavior from the circulator physical hypothesis in the schematic legend.

witnesses.source_id: PRC README and old Thiel project
witnesses.locus: RALA relation and auxiliary reconfiguration circuitry
witnesses.summary: Frames PRC against RALA and describes auxiliary electronics for buffering and processing reconfiguration messages around Universal Cells.
witnesses.simulation_constraint: The first schematic should identify which behavior belongs to the UC core and which belongs to auxiliary stem/reconfiguration support.
witnesses.next_as_action: Document RALA-like auxiliary responsibilities before attempting a dynamic reconfiguration simulation.

witnesses.locus: UC as Mealy machine and activation flowchart
witnesses.summary: Defines the UC as a Mealy machine with wire/proc/stem roles, input/output/upstream channels, memory, automail, control, buffer, and activation flow.
witnesses.simulation_constraint: The first simulation trace should use the existing UC fields and activation vocabulary rather than inventing a new state schema.
witnesses.next_as_action: Map AS's existing Python Cell fields to the PRC formal-model fields before adding any new transition behavior.

witnesses.locus: uc-asm record and wire/proc/stem activation functions
witnesses.summary: Provides an executable Scheme model of UC abstract-state-machine fields and role-specific activation behavior.
witnesses.simulation_constraint: Treat this as an implementation witness but not an unquestioned oracle; AS should preserve its field vocabulary while testing extracted behavior independently.
witnesses.next_as_action: Use the current AS Universal Cell tests as a cleaned-room extraction boundary and add only one schematic-linked trace at a time.

witnesses.summary: Diagram witness for RLEM/GELC node shapes, wire components, and hand-synthesized universal GELC examples.
witnesses.simulation_constraint: The first AS schematic should cite the figure source but redraw only the minimum single-node/port-orientation vocabulary needed for executable trace work.
witnesses.next_as_action: Create a small AS-owned schematic key before attempting a larger GELC circuit diagram.
